twitter_thread: 77% of side hustles fail to generate $1k/month. I've seen 4/5 friends' hustles flop due to poor validation
Validating a business idea can save $10k-$20k in unnecessary expenses and 3-6 months of time
The $100 test: spend $100 on Facebook ads to gauge demand for your product, just like I did with my ecommerce store
Google Trends shows a 300% increase in searches for 'sustainable products' - a niche worth exploring
Scaling too quickly cost one entrepreneur $50k in unsold inventory - a mistake to avoid
Focusing on high-margin services like consulting can increase income by 25% or more
What's the most effective way you've validated a business idea before launch? #incomegeneration #sidehustle
pack.linkedin: I've seen 77% of side hustles fail to generate $1k/month, with 4/5 of my friends' hustles included. Validating a business idea can save $10k-$20k in unnecessary expenses and 3-6 months of time. One approach is to use the $100 test, where you spend $100 on Facebook ads to gauge demand. Google Trends can also help identify profitable niches, like the 300% increase in searches for 'sustainable products'. What's your experience with validating business ideas, and what methods have you found most effective?

pack.reddit_headline: 77% of side hustles fail to generate $1k/month: what's the most effective way to validate a business idea before launch?
